examples.title: Basic File Writing and Reading
examples.description: Demonstrates creating a file, writing data, and reading it back
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates basic file operations: creating a file with ofstream, writing data using << operator, reading data using ifstream and >> operator or getline(). The is_open() function checks if file was successfully opened.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of records

examples.title: Random Access File Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates seeking and tell operations for random access
examples.explanation: This program shows random access in binary files: seekg() positions the read pointer at specific byte offset, tellg() returns current position, and we can read any record directly without sequential traversal. Useful for large databases.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for direct access to any record

examples.title: Binary File Record Update
examples.description: Shows how to update a specific record in a binary file
examples.explanation: Demonstrates updating records in a binary file using fstream with both ios::in and ios::out. After reading, we use seekp() to move back one record and write the updated data. The position calculation is important for correct update.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of records to search

examples.title: File Appending Mode
examples.description: Demonstrates appending data to existing file
examples.explanation: Shows ios::app mode for appending to files. This is commonly used for log files where existing content must be preserved and new entries added at the end. Unlike regular write mode, ios::app doesn't truncate the file.

examples.title: Error Handling in File Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates proper error checking and handling in file I/O
examples.explanation: Demonstrates multiple error handling approaches: is_open() checks if file opened successfully, boolean context checks if stream is valid, and eof()/fail() check the state of the stream during and after operations.
